summary: Native-semantic typography family with three raw heading atoms backed by one core recipe, one shared typography preset, and a shared React/Vue contract.
philosophy: Choose semantic level by document structure first and keep H1, H2, and H3 grounded in real native heading elements.
Keep native semantics as the contract instead of adding Marwes metadata where plain h1/h2/h3 already express the meaning honestly.
Treat size as a deliberate visual escape hatch, not as a shortcut for skipping outline decisions.
Keep heading styling source-owned in theme typography and shared typography.css so Heading and Paragraph stay aligned.

useWhen: The UI needs a real page, section, or subsection heading and document outline should stay explicit.
The product wants React and Vue heading behavior to stay aligned through one core recipe and one shared contract.
Semantic level and visual size may need to diverge intentionally while still keeping the native heading tag.
avoidWhen: The layout only needs visually larger text and does not benefit from real heading semantics.
The surface is really a richer hero, banner, or section-header composition rather than a plain heading atom.
The team expects a family-local metadata layer or purpose-wrapper vocabulary that the current heading family does not ship.

automationCovers: Core recipe tests for semantic tag selection, default size mapping, and explicit size override behavior.
Shared heading contract coverage across React and Vue for native heading semantics, id, and aria-label passthrough.
Storybook introduction and taxonomy tests in both apps for the atom teaching surface.
manualReviewRequired: Product-level judgment about choosing the correct heading level for the real document outline.
Whether size overrides are used sparingly and do not flatten the content hierarchy.
Whether heading ids and anchor-link patterns stay useful and consistent in real documentation or product flows.
notes: Heading completed a first-pass accessibility audit focused on outline-first usage, size override honesty, and native-semantic documentation.
The family now has expanded shared-contract coverage for H1 and the absence of family-local metadata plus explicit accessibility notes in both Storybook apps.

openQuestions: If Marwes later needs H4+, should that extend the current Heading family or arrive as part of a broader typography contract update?
Should the local Figma sync eventually gain a dedicated heading component/page that maps directly to the shipped H1 / H2 / H3 API, or is the typography-page baseline sufficient?
